Welcome to the Wild

Sri Lanka's wild heart beats across diverse landscapes — ancient forests, vast savannas, montane highlands, and untamed coastlines. This 14-day expedition immerses you in the island's greatest wildlife encounters, from witnessing elephant gatherings and leopard sightings to walking with indigenous communities and paddling through serene waterways.

✦

Whether you're cycling through Sigiriya's forest trails, safari-ing across Yala's legendary leopard kingdom, or unwinding on Tangalle's pristine beaches, each day reveals the raw, untamed beauty that makes Sri Lanka a sanctuary for nature lovers and wildlife adventurers.

At a Glance

Signature Experiences

Highlights

Minneriya / Kaudulla National Park — witness wild elephant gatherings
Sigiriya Forest Bike Safari — cycle through ancient forest trails
Kayak Safari on Kala Weva — paddle through wildlife-rich waterways
Gal Oya Boat Safari — the only water-based wildlife safari in Sri Lanka
Walk with the Chief Veddha — discover Sri Lanka's indigenous forest people
Horton Plains & World's End — trekking at 7,000ft with leopard sightings
Yala National Park — home to the world's highest density of leopards
Morning Bush Walk along Yala's wild coastline
Bundala National Park — UNESCO biosphere & flamingo haven
Beach days in Tangalle — Anantara Peace Haven
01
Day 01 · Arrival

Airport — Kotugoda

D
Ayubowan! Welcome to Sri Lanka" — Upon arrival at Bandaranaike International Airport, your OV Holidays representative will greet you and escort you through immigration to the Silk Route Lounge, where you can relax and refresh before your journey begins. Your dedicated chauffeur guide will then transfer you to The Wallawwa, a charming boutique hotel nestled among tranquil gardens just minutes from the airport.
Travel info: Airport — Kotugoda | 9 km | Approx. 20 minutes
Check in and settle in for the evening. Dinner is served at the hotel.
Hotel
The Wallawwa
Garden Suite · Kotugoda · Dinner
02–03
Days 02–03 · Cultural Triangle

Habarana — Wildlife & Village Life

BD
Day 02: After breakfast, check out and depart for Habarana.
Travel: Kotugoda — Habarana | 149 km | Approx. 3 hours
Check in at the hotel. At 14:30, depart for a jeep safari at Minneriya or Kaudulla National Park. Depending on the season, you'll encounter herds of elephants gathering by ancient reservoirs — a truly unforgettable spectacle. With scrub forest, open grassland, and shimmering water as your backdrop, watch as elephants emerge to feed, socialise, and bathe at the water's edge as the sun dips low. Excursion time: approx. 3 hours. Dinner & Overnight: Maia Habarana Day 03: "Sigiriya Forest Bike Safari" — Start: 07:30 AM, Duration: approx. 3 hours. Cycle through Sigiriya Kaley forest trails, home to rich birdlife, monkeys, and other wildlife. Your guide will uncover local myths, wildlife tracking techniques, and natural history as you ride past ancient lakes and village stops. "Optional: Kayak Safari on Kala Weva" — Start: 16:30, Duration: 2 hours. Glide through the waterways of Kala Weva, spotting herons, kingfishers, and the occasional mugger crocodile. Life jackets and safety gear provided. Dinner & Overnight: Maia Habarana
Hotel
Maia Habarana
Superior Room · Habarana · Half Board
04–05
Days 04–05 · Eastern Wilderness

Gal Oya — Sri Lanka's Secret Wild

BLD
Day 04: After breakfast, check out and drive to Gal Oya.
Travel: Habarana — Gal Oya | 183 km | Approx. 3 hrs 45 mins
Arrive and check in at the extraordinary Gal Oya Lodge — nine bungalows set across 20 acres of private forest, constructed from locally sourced natural materials, with a restaurant, bar, and pool overlooking a backdrop of low-lying mountains. This afternoon, embark on the Gal Oya Boat Safari — the only water-based wildlife experience in Sri Lanka. Cruise across the vast Senanayake Samudraya reservoir, where elephants swim between islands, crocodiles bask on rocky shores, and rare endemic birds take flight at dusk. Excursion time: approx. 4 hours. Overnight: Gal Oya Lodge Day 05: "Jungle Drive & Walk Through Gal Oya NP" — Drive into the remote Nilgala section of Gal Oya National Park — once the sacred hunting ground of ancient royalty, rich with endemic wildlife and medicinal flora. Search for herds of elephants, four species of deer, and a remarkable variety of endemic birds. "Walk with the Chief Veddha" — Duration: approx. 2 hours. Gal Oya Lodge neighbours one of the last remaining communities of the Veddha, Sri Lanka's indigenous forest people. The village Chief leads you through the jungle, sharing his tribe's use of medicinal plants, ancient hunting grounds, and cave dwellings. Overnight: Gal Oya Lodge
Hotel
Gal Oya Lodge
Bungalow · Gal Oya · Full Board
06–07
Days 06–07 · Highland Wilderness

Nuwara Eliya — Mist, Mountains & Leopards

BD
Day 06: After breakfast, check out and journey to Nuwara Eliya, Sri Lanka's cool, mist-laden highland capital.
Travel: Gal Oya — Nuwara Eliya | 121 km | Approx. 3 hours
Arrive and settle in at Goatfell, a characterful highland bungalow surrounded by colonial-era gardens. Evening at leisure. Overnight: Goatfell, Nuwara Eliya Day 07: Depart early with a packed breakfast for Horton Plains National Park — a UNESCO World Heritage Site at over 7,000 feet above sea level.
Travel: Nuwara Eliya — Horton Plains | 30 km | Approx. 1.5 hours | Excursion: approx. 4 hours
Trek across frost-kissed grasslands past Baker's Falls and to the edge of World's End — a breathtaking 3,700-foot sheer drop. The plains shelter sambar deer, fishing cats, otters, wild boar, and if fortune favours you, the elusive Sri Lankan Leopard padding silently through the mist. Return to Goatfell upon completion. Overnight: Goatfell, Nuwara Eliya
Hotel
Goatfell
Concordia Bedroom · Nuwara Eliya · Half Board
08–10
Days 08–10 · Wild South

Yala — The Leopard Kingdom

AI
Day 08: After breakfast, check out and journey south to Yala.
Travel: Nuwara Eliya — Yala | 125 km | Approx. 4 hrs 20 mins
Check in at Uga Chena Huts. At 14:30, depart for an afternoon jeep safari through Yala National Park — 1,259 sq km of grassland, scrub forest, and coastline sheltering the world's highest density of wild leopards, alongside elephants, sloth bears, and a spectacular variety of birdlife. Excursion time: approx. 4 hours. Overnight: Uga Chena Huts Day 09: "Morning Bush Walk" — Start: 06:00, Duration: 1.5 hours. Walk Yala's wild coastline at dawn with the resort's expert rangers — through dramatic dunes, rocky outcrops, and untouched beach where sea turtles come to nest. Watch for animal tracks and the signs of nocturnal visitors. Return for breakfast and a restful morning at the resort. "Afternoon Yala Block 5 Safari" — Explore the exclusive, rarely-visited Block 5 — 6,600 hectares of unspoiled wilderness, serene reservoir, and dramatically fewer vehicles. An intimate encounter with Sri Lanka's wildest heart. Overnight: Uga Chena Huts Day 10: Early wake-up at 04:45 for a dawn jeep safari in Bundala National Park — Sri Lanka's first Ramsar wetland and a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. A vibrant mosaic of lagoons, salt pans, and scrub forest, home to nearly 200 bird species including the iconic greater flamingo. Excursion: approx. 4 hours. Return to resort for full breakfast. "Optional: Sithulpawwa Raja Maha Viharaya" — Visit this ancient 2nd century BC Buddhist monastery tucked into rocky outcrops within Yala National Park. A serene, contemplative excursion with sweeping jungle views.
Travel: approx. 45 mins each way | Excursion: approx. 2 hours
Overnight: Uga Chena Huts
Hotel
Uga Chena Huts
Cabin with Pool · Yala · All Inclusive
11–13
Days 11–13 · Coastal Retreat

Tangalle — Sea, Stillness & Southern Shore

B
Day 11: After a leisurely breakfast, check out and drive to Tangalle for a relaxed beach stay.
Travel: Yala — Tangalle | 146 km | Approx. 50 minutes
Check in at Anantara Peace Haven. The afternoon is entirely at leisure. Overnight: Anantara Peace Haven, Tangalle Days 12–13: Two full days of complete freedom. The beach, the pool, and the rhythm of the southern ocean are yours. "Optional: Snorkelling in Tangalle" — Discover vibrant coral gardens and colourful reef fish in the calm, crystal-clear waters along the southern coast. Gentle enough for all experience levels. "Optional: Kayaking in Rekawa Lagoon" — Glide through mangrove-fringed waterways where herons, kingfishers, and water monitors keep quiet company. Best at dawn or late afternoon as the light softens. Overnight: Anantara Peace Haven, Tangalle
Hotel
Anantara Peace Haven
Premier Ocean View Room · Tangalle · Bed & Breakfast
14
Day 14 · Farewell

Tangalle — Airport

B
After a final breakfast, your chauffeur guide will transfer you to Bandaranaike International Airport in time for your departure flight. A packed snack will be provided for the journey.
Travel: Tangalle — Airport | 208 km | Approx. 3 hours
Please be at the airport at least 3 hours prior to your departure flight.
"Thank you for journeying with OV Holidays. We hope Sri Lanka has left its mark on you.

Before You Go

Important Notes

Poya Days
Alcohol service is restricted on religious holidays (Poya Days). Alcohol is not available for purchase at bars or restaurants on these dates.
Check-In & Check-Out
Standard hotel check-in is 14:00. Standard check-out is 12:00. Early check-in and late check-out are subject to availability and may incur additional fees.
Belongings
While we take every precaution, the hotel management is not responsible for lost, stolen, or damaged personal belongings. We recommend using hotel safes.
Travel Times
All travel times are approximate and subject to traffic conditions, road works, and weather. Please allow flexibility in your schedule.
Gratuities
Gratuities for guides and drivers are at your discretion. We recommend presenting tips in an envelope at the end of your journey.
Dress Code
Light, breathable clothing is recommended for Sri Lanka's tropical climate. Comfortable walking shoes are essential. At religious sites, dress modestly (knees to elbows covered).
